hero.title: Live cue cards, routed only through your NullHub
hero.sub: NullCap is a desktop overlay that shows short answer cards on your screen during interviews, meetings, sales calls and demos. It captures screen and audio locally and sends every AI request to exactly one service: NullHub, a gateway running on your own machine at 127.0.0.1:19800 by default.

primary.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
primary.code:
zig build
zig build run -- doctor

quickstart:
NullCap expects a running NullHub at `http://127.0.0.1:19800`. From the repo root:

```bash
# check environment, permissions and NullHub reachability
zig build run -- doctor

# first-run bootstrap: privacy preset, seed workflow profiles
zig build run -- onboarding apply

# pick a session mode
zig build run -- mode meeting

# start the native overlay with global hotkeys
zig build run -- run
```

features.title: Live cue cards
features.body: Short on-screen answers, structures and follow-ups while a call is running. Seven session modes: interview, meeting, demo, sales, debug, system-design, general.

features.title: NullHub-only routing
features.body: Every request — text, image or audio — goes to exactly one address: your local NullHub gateway, 127.0.0.1:19800 by default. NullCap stores no provider API keys and never contacts a model or speech-to-text provider itself; which model actually answers is decided behind the gateway, not in the app.

features.title: Me / other transcript
features.body: Your microphone is transcribed as "me"; system audio — the other side of the call — as "other". With voice-activity detection enabled, clearly silent chunks are dropped locally before anything is transcribed, and long calls are compacted into rolling local summaries so the context never grows without bound.

features.title: Screen capture as evidence
features.body: Full-screen, selected-region and fixed-area capture with prompt-injection guards: text on your screen is treated as evidence, never as instructions.

features.title: Post-call artifacts
features.body: Local summaries, action items, follow-up email drafts and mode-specific scorecards. Tasks export as text, JSON, Jira, Linear or Asana.

features.title: Local-first stores
features.body: Sessions, chats, transcripts and knowledge live under ~/.nullcap with opt-in persistence, retention windows and purge commands. No provider API keys are stored, ever.

note: Experimental: no tagged releases and no license file yet — build from source and expect change.

plain.what: A native desktop overlay app, written in Zig for macOS, Windows and Linux, that floats above your call window showing short AI-generated cue cards, while keeping a live in-memory transcript and generating post-call summaries on demand.
plain.why: Switching to a chatbot mid-call is too slow and hands your conversation to whichever cloud service that chatbot uses; NullCap puts answers on screen in real time and sends every AI request to one place only — your local NullHub gateway, never a model provider directly.
plain.when: Before a job interview, customer demo or sales call: run `nullcap run`, pick one of seven session modes, and get on-screen hints during the call plus a summary, action items and a follow-up email draft after it ends.
